domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides a variety of movement and physical activities for Noah, focusing on his current abilities and encouraging development towards his next milestones.

This activity bank includes engaging and practical activities to support Noah's social and emotional development at home. These activities are designed to be enjoyable and promote interaction, helping him reach his developmental milestones.

Here are some engaging activities to support Noah's language and communication development. These activities are designed to be fun and interactive, helping him practice sounds and reactions in a comfortable home environment.

This activity bank includes engaging and practical activities for Noah to support his cognitive and adaptive development. The activities are designed to be fun and interactive, fitting his current abilities while also encouraging growth towards upcoming milestones.

activities.title: Sound Imitation
activities.description: Make different sounds (like animal noises or silly sounds) and encourage Noah to imitate you. Use a playful tone and smile to keep him engaged.

activities.title: Tummy Time with Toys
activities.description: Place Noah on his tummy on a soft surface. Place colorful toys just out of reach to encourage him to lift his head and reach for them.

activities.title: Cooing Time
activities.description: Lie on the floor with Noah and take turns making cooing sounds. Encourage him to respond to your sounds by making his own.

activities.title: Arm and Leg Movements
activities.description: Lay Noah on his back and gently move his arms and legs in a bicycle motion. Encourage him to try moving them on his own.

activities.title: Sound Reaction Game
activities.description: Use a rattle or a musical toy. Shake it or play it while Noah is looking away, then see if he turns his head towards the sound.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Noah. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical movement and physical activities for Maya, focusing on her current abilities and encouraging her to reach the next developmental milestones.

This activity bank includes engaging and practical activities to support Maya's social and emotional development. The activities are designed to be fun and interactive, helping her connect with you and express her feelings.
This activity bank includes practical language and communication activities for Maya, focusing on her current abilities and encouraging her next steps in development.

This activity bank includes engaging and practical activities to support Maya's cognitive and adaptive skills development at home. The activities are designed to be fun and easy to incorporate into daily routines.

activities.title: Name Calling
activities.description: Call Maya's name from another room and see if she looks for you. Reward her with a smile or clap when she responds.

activities.title: Banging Blocks Together
activities.description: Provide Maya with two soft blocks. Show her how to bang them together, then encourage her to try it herself.

activities.title: Peek-a-Boo
activities.description: Play peek-a-boo by hiding your face behind your hands or a cloth and then revealing yourself. Encourage Maya to do the same.

activities.title: Hide and Seek with a Toy
activities.description: While Maya watches, hide a small toy under a blanket. Encourage her to find it by lifting the blanket.

activities.title: Facial Expression Game
activities.description: Make different facial expressions (happy, sad, surprised) and encourage Maya to mimic you. Use a mirror to help her see her own expressions.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Maya. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

concerns_bullets: Limited eye contact and few words

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank includes a variety of movement and physical activities designed to support Leo's development in a fun and engaging way. The activities focus on current skills and encourage progression towards next milestones.
This activity bank includes engaging and practical activities to support Leo's social and emotional development, focusing on play, affection, and interaction with others.

This activity bank provides practical and engaging activities to support Leo's language and communication skills at home. The activities focus on current-level practice and near next-step practice to encourage progress in a fun and interactive way.

This activity bank provides practical and engaging activities for Leo to support his cognitive and adaptive skills at home. The activities are designed to be short and manageable, promoting both current-level practice and near next-step skills.

activities.title: Excited Clapping
activities.description: Whenever Leo shows excitement, model clapping your hands and encourage him to join in. You can use a favorite toy or activity to elicit excitement.

activities.title: Couch Climbing
activities.description: Encourage Leo to climb on and off the couch or a sturdy chair. You can use pillows to create a safe climbing area.

activities.title: Pat-a-Cake Game
activities.description: Sit facing Leo and sing the 'Pat-a-Cake' song while clapping your hands together. Encourage him to clap along with you.

activities.title: Cup Drinking Practice
activities.description: Fill a cup with a small amount of water and let Leo practice drinking from it. Be ready for spills and encourage him to try again.

activities.title: Copying Play
activities.description: Play with a container and some toys. Take a toy out and encourage Leo to copy you by taking a toy out as well. Use simple phrases like 'Your turn!'

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Leo. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical movement and physical activities for Sofia, focusing on developing her gross motor skills and fine motor abilities in a fun and engaging way.

This activity bank provides engaging and practical activities to support Sofia's social and emotional development at home, focusing on her current abilities and encouraging growth towards next milestones.

This activity bank provides engaging and practical activities to support Sofia's language and communication skills at home. The activities are designed to be fun and fit her developmental level while encouraging progress towards her milestone targets.

This activity bank provides practical and engaging activities for Sofia to support her cognitive and adaptive skills development. The activities are designed to be fun and fit within the home environment, focusing on both current-level and near next-step practices.

activities.title: Body Part Show and Tell
activities.description: Ask Sofia to show you different body parts like 'Where's your nose?' or 'Show me your feet.' Celebrate her responses!

activities.title: Emotion Faces Game
activities.description: Make different facial expressions (happy, sad, surprised) and encourage Sofia to mimic them. Talk about what each expression means.

activities.title: Gesture Play
activities.description: Play a game where you model different gestures like blowing a kiss or nodding. Encourage Sofia to imitate you.

activities.title: Clean Up Time
activities.description: At the end of playtime, say 'It's clean-up time!' and help Sofia pick up toys together. Encourage her to follow your lead.

activities.title: Two-Word Requests
activities.description: During snack time, encourage Sofia to use two words together, like 'more juice' or 'want cookie.' Prompt her gently if needed.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Sofia. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides a variety of movement and physical activities for Ethan, focusing on both current skills and near next-step practices to support his development in a fun and engaging way.

This activity bank provides engaging and practical social and emotional activities for Ethan, focusing on his current level and near next-step skills. These activities aim to enhance his ability to interact with peers, manage emotions, and understand social cues.

This activity bank includes engaging and practical language and communication activities for Ethan, focusing on his current level and near next-step skills. These activities are designed to fit into daily routines and promote language development in a fun way.
This activity bank provides practical and engaging activities for Ethan to support his cognitive and adaptive skills development at home.

activities.title: Spoon Practice
activities.description: Give Ethan a bowl of soft food and a spoon. Encourage him to eat independently while you supervise. Offer help as needed.

activities.title: Emotion Charades
activities.description: Take turns acting out different emotions (happy, sad, angry) while the other guesses. Discuss what makes you feel each emotion.

activities.title: Jumping Fun
activities.description: Create a safe space with soft mats or cushions. Encourage Ethan to jump off the ground with both feet, celebrating each jump.

activities.title: Calm Down Corner
activities.description: Create a calm down space with pillows and soft toys. Practice going there together when feeling upset, and talk about how to calm down.

activities.title: Kick the Ball
activities.description: Set up a soft ball in a safe area. Encourage Ethan to kick the ball towards a target, like a box or a wall.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Ethan. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides engaging movement and physical activities for Amina, focusing on her current abilities and helping her progress towards her developmental milestones.

This activity bank provides engaging and practical activities to support Amina's social and emotional development, focusing on her current abilities and encouraging growth towards her next milestones.

This activity bank provides engaging and practical language and communication activities for Amina, focusing on her current level and near next-step skills. These activities are designed to enhance her language development in a fun and manageable way.
This activity bank provides engaging and practical activities for Amina to support her cognitive and adaptive skills at home, focusing on her current level and near next-step practices.

activities.title: Container Lid Play
activities.description: Give Amina a container with a lid and some small toys. Encourage her to hold the container in one hand while using the other hand to take the lid off and put the toys inside.

activities.title: Playdate Invitation
activities.description: Encourage Amina to ask a neighbor or friend to play. Help her formulate the question, like 'Can I play with Alex?'

activities.title: Color Sorting
activities.description: Provide Amina with a set of colored blocks or crayons. Ask her to point to or hand you a specific color when you name it, like 'Can you show me the blue one?'

activities.title: Comforting Friends
activities.description: Use dolls or stuffed animals to role-play comforting a sad character. Encourage Amina to hug or talk to the character.

activities.title: Two-Step Instructions Game
activities.description: Play a game where you give Amina two simple instructions to follow, such as 'Put the toy down and clap your hands.'

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Amina. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical and engaging activities to support Lucas's movement and physical development, focusing on his current abilities and encouraging growth towards next milestones.
This activity bank provides a variety of short, engaging activities to support Lucas's social and emotional development, focusing on interaction and connection.
This activity bank provides engaging and practical activities to support Lucas's language and communication development at home, focusing on current skills and near next steps.
This activity bank provides practical home activities for Lucas to support his cognitive and adaptive development, focusing on current-level and near next-step skills.

activities.title: Happy Greetings
activities.description: Practice greeting Lucas with a big smile and a cheerful voice when you enter the room. Observe his reaction and encourage him to smile back.

activities.title: Cooing Back and Forth
activities.description: Sit face-to-face with Lucas. Make cooing sounds like 'oooo' or 'aahhh' and encourage him to mimic you. Wait for him to respond, then repeat the sounds back to him.

activities.title: Face Time
activities.description: Sit in front of Lucas and make different facial expressions. Encourage him to look at your face and mimic your expressions. Use a mirror if available.

activities.title: Sound Turn-Taking
activities.description: Take turns making sounds with Lucas. You can squeal, clap, or make funny noises. Encourage him to imitate you and respond to your sounds.

activities.title: Silly Sounds
activities.description: Make silly sounds or noises to get Lucas's attention. Pause to see if he responds with sounds or looks your way.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Lucas. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank includes a variety of movement and physical activities designed for Emma, focusing on her current abilities and encouraging her to reach the next developmental milestones.
This activity bank includes practical social and emotional activities designed for Emma, focusing on her current developmental level while also encouraging her to reach near next-step milestones.

This activity bank includes practical language and communication activities for Emma, focusing on her current skills and near next steps. These activities are designed to be engaging and suitable for home practice.

This activity bank provides a variety of engaging and practical home activities for Emma, focusing on cognitive and adaptive skills. The activities are designed to support her current abilities while encouraging growth towards developmental milestones.

activities.title: Wave Goodbye
activities.description: Practice waving goodbye during daily routines. Use a toy or a family member to model waving and encourage Emma to join in.

activities.title: Washing Hands Together
activities.description: Make handwashing fun by singing a short song while you wash Emma's hands together. Encourage her to hold out her hands for you.

activities.title: Name Game
activities.description: Sit with Emma and look at family photos. Point to each person and say their name. Encourage Emma to repeat or gesture to indicate who she recognizes.

activities.title: Dress-Up Fun
activities.description: Let Emma help you dress by guiding her to push her arm through a sleeve or lift her foot for a shoe. Make it playful by using a favorite stuffed animal to dress up too.

activities.title: Follow the Gesture
activities.description: Use simple gestures paired with words. For example, say 'come here' while gesturing for her to come. Encourage her to follow your gesture.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Emma. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank includes a variety of movement and physical activities designed to support Zain's development in a fun and engaging way. The activities focus on current skills and near next-step skills to help him practice self-regulation and coordination.

This activity bank provides a variety of social and emotional activities tailored for Zain, focusing on current-level and near next-step practices to support his development in a fun and engaging way.

This activity bank includes engaging language and communication activities tailored for Zain, focusing on both current skills and next-step development. Each activity is designed to be practical and fun, encouraging language use in everyday situations.
This activity bank provides engaging and practical activities for Zain to support cognitive and adaptive skills development, focusing on current-level and near next-step practices.

activities.title: Emotion Faces Game
activities.description: Make different facial expressions (happy, sad, surprised) and encourage Zain to mimic them. Talk about how each expression might feel.

activities.title: Book Naming
activities.description: Choose a picture book and point to different objects. Ask Zain, 'What is this?' and encourage him to name the items.

activities.title: Clean Up Time Song
activities.description: Sing a clean-up song while picking up toys together. Encourage Zain to help by following your lead when you say it's clean-up time.

activities.title: Action Words Game
activities.description: Use toys or stuffed animals to act out simple actions. Say a phrase like 'doggie run' and encourage Zain to repeat it while mimicking the action.

activities.title: Emotion Matching Cards
activities.description: Create or use cards with different facial expressions. Take turns matching the cards and discussing the emotions shown.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Zain. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank includes a variety of movement and physical activities designed to support Isla's development in a fun and engaging way. The activities focus on current-level skills and near next-step skills to promote progress in her physical abilities.
This activity bank includes engaging social and emotional activities tailored for Isla, focusing on her current developmental level and encouraging growth towards her next milestones.
This activity bank includes engaging and practical activities to support Isla's language and communication skills, focusing on her current level and near next steps.

This activity bank includes a variety of cognitive and adaptive activities designed to support Isla's developmental milestones in a fun and engaging way. Each activity is tailored to her current abilities while also encouraging her to reach for the next steps.

activities.title: Body Parts Game
activities.description: Ask Isla to show you different body parts by saying, 'Where's your nose?' or 'Show me your feet!'. Start with familiar body parts and gradually introduce new ones.

activities.title: Spoon Practice
activities.description: Provide Isla with a bowl of soft food (like yogurt or applesauce) and a spoon. Encourage her to scoop and eat independently. Offer help if needed, but let her try on her own.

activities.title: Simple Directions
activities.description: Give Isla simple one-step directions like 'Come here!' or 'Give me the ball!'. Encourage her to follow without gestures.

activities.title: Ball Kicking Fun
activities.description: Set up a soft ball in a safe space. Encourage Isla to kick the ball back and forth with you. Start by rolling it to her and let her kick it back.

activities.title: Gesture Play
activities.description: Play a game where you model different gestures like blowing a kiss or nodding. Encourage Isla to imitate you and use gestures during play.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Isla. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank includes practical movement and physical activities for Aria to support her development at home.
Here are six engaging activities to support Aria's social and emotional development, focusing on current skills and near-next steps.
This activity bank provides engaging and practical activities to support Aria's language and communication development at home.
Here are six engaging activities to support Aria's cognitive and adaptive skills at home.

activities.title: Toy Naming Game
activities.description: Hold up a familiar toy and say its name. Encourage Aria to look at the toy when you name it. Repeat with different toys.

activities.title: Couch Climbing
activities.description: Encourage Aria to climb on and off the couch safely. You can place soft cushions on the floor for safety.

activities.title: Follow the Direction
activities.description: Give a simple one-step direction like 'give me the ball' without gestures. Praise her when she follows the direction.

activities.title: Cup Drinking Practice
activities.description: Fill a cup with a small amount of water and let Aria practice drinking from it. Expect some spills!

activities.title: Point and Ask
activities.description: Place a few toys out of reach. Encourage Aria to point to a toy she wants and say 'that' or 'give.'

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Aria. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank includes a variety of movement and physical activities designed for Mateo, focusing on current skills and next-step development. These activities are practical for home and aim to support his physical milestones.

Here are some engaging activities to support Mateo's social and emotional development. These activities are designed to encourage interaction, communication, and exploration in a fun and practical way.

This activity bank provides practical and engaging activities to support Mateo's language and communication skills at home. The activities are designed to fit his current developmental level while encouraging growth towards near next-step milestones.

This activity bank provides practical and engaging activities for Mateo to support his cognitive and adaptive development. The activities are designed to be fun and fit into a typical home environment, promoting skills that align with his current developmental level and next steps.

activities.title: Bye-Bye Wave
activities.description: Practice waving bye-bye together. Encourage Mateo to wave when you say 'bye-bye' to toys or family members.

activities.title: Dress-Up Fun
activities.description: Let Mateo help you dress him by guiding his arms through the sleeves of a shirt or lifting his feet for shoes. Make it playful by using a favorite toy or character as a model.

activities.title: Name Game
activities.description: Sit with Mateo and hold up a picture of a family member or a favorite toy. Say the name clearly and encourage him to repeat it. If he doesn’t, model it a few times and encourage him to point to the picture or toy.

activities.title: Hide and Seek Glance
activities.description: Play a simple game of hide and seek where you hide briefly and then peek out. Encourage Mateo to look for you and check in with him to see if he is looking.

activities.title: Follow the Gesture
activities.description: Give Mateo simple directions using both a gesture and words, like 'come here' while waving your hand. Praise him when he follows the direction.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Mateo. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical movement and physical activities for Luna, focusing on her current skills and near next-step goals. Each activity is designed to be short and manageable for busy parents while promoting her physical development.
This activity bank provides practical and engaging activities to support Luna's social and emotional development, focusing on her current abilities and near next steps.

This activity bank includes engaging language and communication activities tailored for Luna's developmental level. Each activity is designed to be short and practical for home, focusing on enhancing her speech and understanding.
This activity bank provides practical and engaging activities for Luna to support her cognitive and adaptive skills development, focusing on her current level and near next steps.

activities.title: Look at Me!
activities.description: Encourage Luna to show you something she can do (like a dance move or a drawing) and say 'Look at me!' when she does.

activities.title: Toy Sorting
activities.description: Gather a few different toys of various colors. Ask Luna to sort them by color into different containers. You can help her by naming the colors as she sorts.

activities.title: Emotion Faces
activities.description: Make different facial expressions (happy, sad, surprised) and encourage Luna to mimic them. Talk about what each face means.

activities.title: Two-Step Instructions
activities.description: Give Luna simple two-step instructions, like 'Pick up the ball and put it in the box.' Praise her when she follows the instructions.

activities.title: Clean Up Time
activities.description: At the end of playtime, say 'It's clean-up time!' and guide Luna to help put away toys. Praise her for helping.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Luna. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: We do not think Ryan has a meaningful delay in Movement / Physical and may not need special support in this category right now.

This activity bank includes practical and engaging activities to support Ryan's social and emotional development. The activities are designed to help him manage transitions, engage with peers, and develop empathy.
This activity bank provides practical and engaging activities to support Ryan's language and communication skills, focusing on both current-level and near next-step practices.

This activity bank provides a variety of engaging and practical activities to support Ryan's cognitive and adaptive skills at home. The activities are designed to be fun and encourage learning through play, while also addressing the developmental milestones relevant to his age.

activities.title: Who Am I?
activities.description: Ask Ryan to tell you his first name when you say, 'Who are you?'. Then, ask him to ask you the same question.

activities.title: Emotion Charades
activities.description: Take turns acting out different emotions (happy, sad, angry) while the other guesses. Discuss what makes you feel that way.

activities.title: Question Time
activities.description: Take turns asking each other simple questions. Start with questions like 'What is a coat for?' or 'What do we use a crayon for?'.

activities.title: Transition Countdown
activities.description: Use a timer to help Ryan transition between activities. Explain what will happen next and set the timer for 5 minutes.

activities.title: Picture Storytime
activities.description: Look at a picture book together. Ask Ryan what action is happening in different pictures, like 'What is he doing?' or 'What is she eating?'.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Social / Emotional, Language / Communication, Cognitive / Adaptive for Ryan. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed in the interview.
